name: Garlic Chutney
description: Garlic Chutney, also known as Poondu Chutney or Lehsun Ki Chutney, is a spicy and pungent South Indian condiment made with garlic, dry red chillies, coconut and tamarind. The chillies are soaked, ground with garlic into a paste, and then slow-cooked until the raw smell disappears. A simple tempering of mustard and curry leaves finishes this bold, flavour-packed chutney that pairs beautifully with idli, dosa and upma.

steps.instruction: Boil 1 cup water. Add dry red chillies and let them soak for 10 to 15 minutes.
steps.tip: Use Kashmiri red chillies for a bright red colour and milder heat level.

steps.instruction: Cool completely, then drain the red chillies. Reserve the soaked water.
To a mixer jar, add drained red chillies, garlic cloves and tamarind.
Add a little of the reserved soaked water and grind to a slightly fine paste.
Heat ½ tbsp gingelly oil in a small kadai. Add the chilli-garlic paste.
steps.tip: Gingelly oil is preferred for this recipe as it gives a nice flavour and balances the spice.

steps.instruction: Rinse the mixer jar with ¼ cup water and add it to the kadai.
Mix well. When it starts to boil, cook partially covered (it splutters a lot).
Open, give a quick mix, then cook covered partially again. Repeat until the raw smell of garlic leaves — about 10 minutes. The chutney will thicken.
steps.tip: Cooking until the raw smell of garlic completely disappears is crucial — do not rush this step.

steps.instruction: Add required salt and jaggery. Mix well.
steps.tip: Jaggery balances the spice level and adds a subtle sweetness.

steps.instruction: Add ½ tbsp gingelly oil, mix and cook for 2 more minutes.
For tempering, heat 1 tbsp gingelly oil in a tadka pan. Add mustard seeds and curry leaves. Let them splutter.
Add tempering to the chutney. Give a quick mix and switch off.
Serve hot with idli, dosa, upma, oothapam or vada.
steps.tip: This chutney can be stored in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 days. For a spread-style chutney, cook until very thick and oil separates.

tips: Use Kashmiri red chillies for a bright red colour and milder heat level.
Gingelly oil is preferred for this recipe as it gives a nice flavour and balances the spice.
Jaggery balances the spice level and adds a subtle sweetness.
Cooking until the raw smell of garlic completely disappears is crucial — do not rush this step.
This chutney can be stored in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 days.
For a spread-style chutney, cook until very thick and oil separates.
variations: Dry Garlic Chutney (Lehsun ki Chutney with desiccated coconut)
